input.prompt:
I'm starting a new console app on .NET 11 and I need to serialize this record to JSON:

```csharp
public record Person(string name, int age);
```

In the output JSON the property names should be PascalCase (so `Name` and `Age`,
not `name`/`age` or `firstName` style). I want to use whatever the framework
provides out of the box rather than writing my own naming-policy class.

Please show me the full program targeting `net11.0` and run it so I can see the
JSON output.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new console app on .NET 11 and I need to serialize this record to JSON:

```csharp
public record Person(string name, int age);
```

In the output JSON the property names should be PascalCase (so `Name` and `Age`,
not `name`/`age` or `firstName` style). I want to use whatever the framework
provides out of the box rather than writing my own naming-policy class.

Please show me the full program targeting `net11.0` and run it so I can see the
JSON output.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants a .NET 11 console app that serializes a `Person` record to JSON with PascalCase property names using built-in framework features.

In .NET 11 (which would be released in November 2026), I need to check what's available. Let me think about what I know:

- In .NET 8, `System.Text.Json` added `JsonNamingPolicy.SnakeCaseLower`, `JsonNamingPolicy.SnakeCaseUpper`, `JsonNamingPolicy.KebabCaseLower`, `JsonNamingPolicy.KebabCaseUpper`.


- `JsonNamingPolicy.CamelCase` has been available since .NET Core 3.0, but there's no built-in `PascalCase` policy that I'm aware of.

The default behavior of `System.Text.Json` preserves property names as-is, so if the record uses lowercase parameter names like `Person(string name, int age)`, the JSON output will have lowercase properties. I need to reconsider what the user is actually asking for here. Since we're in May 2026, .NET 11 would still be in preview, so I need to check what SDKs are actually available on this machine. The core issue is that the record has lowercase property names but the user wants PascalCase JSON output using only built-in framework features. The `[JsonPropertyName]` attribute would be the straightforward approach to map each property to its PascalCase equivalent. Let me check what .NET version is available and whether `JsonNamingPolicy.PascalCase` exists.

data.content: .NET 11 preview 3 is available. Let me scaffold the project and check what's built-in for PascalCase naming.
